name: Guava and Cheese Cornbread
intro: You might be thinking, Why guava cornbread? Well, why not? Guava does great with savory flavors, and since cornbread isn’t that sweet, the guava adds sweetness, making it the perfect sweet and savory brunch side. Being Black Rican and vegan, I often think of flavors that I can get from the best of all those worlds, and I incorporate them to make magic. Just as my mom and dad did with me. LOL.
method: In a medium-sized bowl, whisk together your cornmeal, flour, cane sugar, baking powder and teaspoon of salt. In a small bowl, combine the ground flaxseeds and water. Mix and set aside to thicken. This will be your flax egg.

In a separate medium-sized bowl, combine the ¼ cup (80 g) of guava jelly, melted vegan butter, avocado oil, flax egg and nondairy milk, and whisk everything together. Add the guava mixture to the cornmeal mixture and combine, but do not overmix.

Oil your 8-inch (20-cm)-square brownie pan with coconut oil. Pour in your cornbread mixture. Lightly top the mixture with the remaining tablespoon (20 g) of guava jelly and swirl it into the mixture. Bake for 15 minutes, or until golden brown.
In a small bowl, combine the vegan butter, rosemary and guava jelly.
Serve the cornbread hot and spread the guava butter on top.
